title: Follow handler: follower inbox domain not validated against actor domain
description:
src/server.rs, handle_inbox Follow handler (~line 985)

When a Follow is received, the actor document is fetched from follower_actor URI, and inbox_uri is extracted from actor_doc["inbox"]. There is no check that inbox_uri is on the same domain as follower_actor. A malicious server could advertise inbox = https://victim.example/inbox in their actor document, causing broadside to deliver Accept activities (signed with our private key) to an arbitrary third-party server of the attacker's choosing.

The SSRF guard (lines 1006-1012) blocks private IPs but does not prevent delivery to unrelated public domains. This enables delivery misdirection: broadside sends signed AP messages to a server it has no relationship with, which could be used for spam amplification or AP message forgery at scale.

Fix: validate that url::Url::parse(&inbox_uri).host_str() == url::Url::parse(follower_actor).host_str() and reject if they differ.
close_reason: Fixed: Follow handler validates inbox host matches follower actor host
